title: Power Play Crease
description: Slide a goalie glove across the crease to snag Canucks shots, slap away Predators traps, and ride hot streaks before three breakaways get through.

howToPlayJson.title: Power Play Crease
howToPlayJson.objective: Score as many points as possible in a 3-minute match by catching blue Canucks pucks, avoiding gold Predators decoys, and chaining clean saves into a multiplier.

controls.input: Drag left or right with one thumb along the bottom third of the screen
controls.outcome: Repositions the catcher under falling shots and traps
Pops the glove upward for a short swat that destroys one dangerous Predators item

controls.input: Press and hold with one thumb
controls.outcome: Shrinks movement but widens catch radius for dense Canucks shot waves

steps: Blue Canucks pucks and gold Predators traps rain toward the crease from the top of the screen.
Catch blue pucks to score and build a combo; every clean catch within the combo window increases the multiplier.
Avoid gold traps, predator claws, and fake wobble-pucks; touching them breaks the combo and costs one life.
Every 8 seconds the rink horn triggers an Ice Tilt twist: the lane lights flip and only one half of the rink is safe for catches while the
During Ice Tilt, shots landing on the lit half are worth double, but crossing into the unlit half risks a fast Predators ambush drop.
Speed, fake-outs, and mixed patterns intensify over time until the 3-minute clock ends or the player loses all 3 lives.
howToPlayJson.scoring: Blue puck catch: 10 points. Clean streak multiplier rises from x1 to x5 after consecutive catches. Swatting a gold trap at the last second grants 25 points and freezes combo decay for 2 seconds. Missing a blue puck does
tips: Stay in the lower center so one-thumb drags can reach both corners.
Use the tap swat only on unavoidable gold traps; dragging is safer for maintaining combos.
When Ice Tilt flips, commit early to the lit side because late crossovers often run into ambush drops.
Hold stance during dense Canucks barrages to widen the glove, then release to recover mobility.
